question_text: Explain why fossil fuels are considered non-renewable energy resources, and give one consequence of relying on them for electricity generation.
correct_answer: Fossil fuels are non-renewable because they take millions of years to form and we use them much faster than they can be replaced. One consequence is that burning them releases carbon dioxide, contributing to climate change and global warming.
explanation: This tests understanding of what makes a resource non-renewable (the timescale issue) and the environmental impact of fossil fuel combustion. Students could also mention resource depletion, air pollution, or habitat destruction as valid consequences.
